steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: incomplete participial phrase “commencing Mission”
After the blank: a noun phrase (the mission designation), then a time phrase, then an independent clause: “the mission ended when the shuttle landed...”

steps.title: Apply the rule for separating independent clauses
steps.content:
Choice D applies the rule: A period separates two independent clauses. By placing a period after “STS-53,” D properly ends the first sentence and begins a second sentence: “Lasting seven days and seven hours, the mission ended...” (participial phrase introducing the main clause).

Options A, B, and C violate this rule by attempting to keep both independent clauses in a single sentence, connected only by commas.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
